input.query: Python asyncio event loop best practices

results.title: Asyncio — Python 3.12 documentation
results.snippet: asyncio is a library to write concurrent code using the async/await syntax. asyncio is used as a foundation for multiple Python asynchronous frameworks.

results.title: Python Asyncio: The Complete Guide — Super Fast Python
results.snippet: Python asyncio provides the foundation for writing single-threaded concurrent programs using coroutines, event loops, and futures.

results.title: Coroutines and Tasks — Python 3.12 documentation
results.snippet: Coroutines declared with the async/await syntax are the preferred way of writing asyncio applications. The event loop runs at the heart of every asyncio application.

input.query: ruff python linter vs flake8 performance benchmark

results.title: Ruff: An Extremely Fast Python Linter
results.snippet: Ruff is an extremely fast Python linter and code formatter, written in Rust. It is designed to be a drop-in replacement for Flake8, isort, and many other tools.

results.title: Ruff vs Flake8 — which Python linter should you use?
results.snippet: Ruff is 10-100x faster than Flake8 because it is implemented in Rust. It also implements rules from many other linters (pyflakes, pycodestyle, isort) in a single binary.

results.title: Benchmarks — Ruff documentation
results.snippet: Ruff lints the CPython codebase in approximately 0.3 seconds — over 100x faster than Flake8.

input.query: GitHub Actions OpenID Connect OIDC AWS role assumption tutorial

results.title: Configuring OpenID Connect in Amazon Web Services — GitHub Docs
results.snippet: You can use GitHub's OIDC provider with AWS to assume a role and deploy to AWS without storing long-lived AWS credentials as GitHub Actions secrets.

results.title: Using OpenID Connect with GitHub Actions — AWS Security Blog
results.snippet: GitHub Actions supports OpenID Connect (OIDC), which allows your workflows to request short-lived credentials from AWS by assuming an IAM role using a web identity token.

results.title: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials — GitHub
results.snippet: This action sets up AWS credential environment variables for use in subsequent steps. Supports OIDC-based role assumption, static credentials, and web identity token files.
